sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The log roll is a coordinated turning manoeuvre used when a patient needs to be examined, repositioned, or moved while limiting unnecessary spinal movement. It should be used deliberately, not routinely, because every roll carries some movement and time cost.

sections.title: Procedure (3+ Rescuers)
sections.content:
1. Assign a team leader at the head to coordinate timing and alignment

2. Position the team so the shoulders, torso, pelvis, and legs can be moved together

3. Prepare all equipment before rolling so the patient is turned the minimum number of times

4. Roll the patient in one controlled movement on the leader's command

5. Perform the needed inspection or packaging efficiently, then return or transfer the patient in a coordinated way

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- The team leader controls timing and should stop the roll if alignment or safety is being lost
- Avoid repeated log rolls when a simpler or safer movement strategy exists
- Suspected unstable pelvic injury may require an alternative transfer method
- Document why the roll was needed and what was found
